title: Support exposing coverage counters through C++ API
body:
### My Use Case
I would like to read and process coverage counts in my C++ test bench many times during a single simulation run. Currently I write the coverage data to disk before parsing + processing it and my test bench spends ~90% of the time in that part of the code.

### Current Capabilities

I have had a look at the `verilated_cov.h` file that comes with Verilator. It currently supports zeroing out the coverage counts as well as writing coverage counts to a file.

### What would you like added/supported?
I would like to be able to programmatically read out the coverage counts with as little overhead as possible. For my use-case, an API that allows me to discover the pointers to all coverage counters once would be sufficient. I am also fine with dumping the coverage counters to a file once in order to get all the meta-data. After that I would want to access the counters directly through memory without going to disk.

### May we assist you in trying to fix this yourself?

What kind of API would you prefer? Personally I would be OK with a single method added to `VerilatedCovImp` that returns all cover counters in the order in which they will be written to the coverage file as a `std::vector<uint32_t*>`. I can see how that would be a little too hacky though so I am happy to hear your thoughts.

Thanks!
